name: nillion-client-proto
documentation:
# Python Nillion client

Nillion-client is a Python client for building on top of the Nillion Network.
It can be used to manage Nada programs, store and retrieve values, and run
computations.

See the official Nillion documentation site for more about [Nillion
Clients][clients], [Python Client Examples][examples] and the [Python Client
Reference][reference].

[clients]: https://docs.nillion.com/nillion-client

[examples]: https://docs.nillion.com/python-client-examples

[reference]: https://docs.nillion.com/python-client-reference

classes.name: nillion_client_proto.nillion.compute.v1.ComputeStub

methods.name: nillion_client_proto.nillion.compute.v1.ComputeStub.invoke_compute
documentation: Invoke a computation.

methods.name: nillion_client_proto.nillion.compute.v1.ComputeStub.stream_compute
documentation:
Start a stream of message for a specific instance of a computation.

 This is meant to be used internally by nodes in the network.

methods.name: nillion_client_proto.nillion.compute.v1.ComputeStub.retrieve_results
documentation:
Retrieve the result of a computation.

 The user invoking this operation must have been defined as an output party in the program
 the execution was tied to.

 This call will wait for the program execution to end if it hasn't done so already. Clients must
 wait until either a result or failure message is sent, ignoring any `Waiting` messages sent until
 that happens.
A service to perform compute operations.

documentation: Invoke a computation.

methods.name: nillion_client_proto.nillion.compute.v1.ComputeBase.stream_compute
documentation:
Start a stream of message for a specific instance of a computation.

 This is meant to be used internally by nodes in the network.

methods.name: nillion_client_proto.nillion.compute.v1.ComputeBase.retrieve_results
documentation:
Retrieve the result of a computation.

 The user invoking this operation must have been defined as an output party in the program
 the execution was tied to.

 This call will wait for the program execution to end if it hasn't done so already. Clients must
 wait until either a result or failure message is sent, ignoring any `Waiting` messages sent until
 that happens.
A service to perform compute operations.

class_variables.name: nillion_client_proto.nillion.auth.v1.token.Token.nonce
documentation:
A nonce that adds entropy to this token.

 The nonce must be 32 bytes long and will be considered invalid otherwise.

class_variables.name: nillion_client_proto.nillion.auth.v1.token.Token.target_identity
documentation:
The identifier of the node this token is meant to be used against.

 This needs to match the target node's identity for the token to be considered valid, and is used to prevent
 a node from taking the token and impersonating the user.

class_variables.name: nillion_client_proto.nillion.auth.v1.token.Token.expires_at
documentation: The timestamp at which this token expires.
A token.

documentation: An ED25519 public key.

classes.name: nillion_client_proto.nillion.auth.v1.public_key.PublicKey

documentation: The type of this public key.

class_variables.name: nillion_client_proto.nillion.auth.v1.public_key.PublicKey.contents
documentation:
The contents of this key.

 This is dependent on the `key_type` in this key:

 * For ED25519 it should be the curve point in its 32 byte compressed form.
 * For SECP256K1 it should be the curve point in its 33 byte compressed form.

A public key.

 Signatures associated to this public key must be a 64 bytes long sequence which is the result of the
 concatenation of the `r` and `s` scalars in the signature, each encoded in big endian.

class_variables.name: nillion_client_proto.nillion.values.v1.store.StoreValuesRequest.permissions
documentation:
The permissions to set for these values.

 Permissions are required when storing values and optional when updating them. That is,
 if `update_identifier` is set then this attribute is optional.

class_variables.name: nillion_client_proto.nillion.values.v1.store.StoreValuesRequest.update_identifier
documentation:
The optional values identifier to use when storing these values.

 The given identifier must be a valid values identifier that the invoking user has update permissions
 on for this operation to be considered valid.

 Setting this identifier will effectively turn this operation into an update for an existing set of
 values.
A request to store a set of values.

documentation: The compute permissions to be granted/revoked.

A request to update the permissions on a set of values to another user.

 Any `revoke_*` permissions only have an effect if the user id provided already had
 that permission before. e.g. using `revoke_retrieve` for a user id that doesn't have
 retrieve permissions has no effect.
